title: Dodecatheon utahense
taxonomy: Dodecatheon utahense is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the western United States and is also known as the Utah shooting star. The genus Dodecatheon is a group of flowering plants commonly found in the Pacific Northwest region of North America. Within the Primulaceae family, Dodecatheon utahense is one of the several species in the genus Dodecatheon. The classification of Dodecatheon utahense is further supported by its distinctive bell-shaped flowers and unique growth habits.

intro: Dodecatheon utahense, also known as Utah shootingstar, is a perennial wildflower native to the mountainous regions of the western United States, characterized by its delicate, bell-shaped flowers and slender stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing symptoms of respiratory infections and wounds. The bioactive constituents of Dodecatheon utahense include iridoids, phenolic acids, and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Dodecatheon utahense is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as to promote wound healing.
